
## Motivating example: `birthweight` data

Research question: is there really an interaction between sex and age?

$H_0: \beta_{AM} = 0$

$H_A: \beta_{AM} \neq 0$

$P(|\hat\beta_{AM}| > |`r coef(bw_lm2)["sexmale:age"]`| \mid H_0)$ = ?

## Inference for individual predictor coefficients

{{< include _subfiles/Linear-models-overview/_sec_linreg_coef_sampling_dist.qmd >}}

---

### Estimated covariance matrix and standard errors

{{< include _subfiles/Linear-models-overview/_sec_bw_vcov.qmd >}}

### Wald tests and confidence intervals

{{< include _subfiles/Linear-models-overview/_sec_linreg_wald.qmd >}}

## Inference for predicted means

{{< include _subfiles/Linear-models-overview/_sec_linreg_pred_inference.qmd >}}

## Inference for differences in means

{{< include _subfiles/Linear-models-overview/_sec_linreg_diff_means_inference.qmd >}}
